description_long: This house features a simple rectangular structure with a steeply pitched red gable roof and a secondary gable extension. The exterior walls are clad in horizontal light wood planks, while the foundation is constructed from reddish-brown brick. One side of the house includes a large open porch area supported by wooden posts, providing a sheltered entryway. The roof overhangs the porch, creating a prominent covered space. There are no visible windows or doors on most sides, except for the open porch area, which suggests the main entrance is located there. The overall design is utilitarian and rustic, reminiscent of traditional rural or cabin-style homes.
description: A rustic, wood-clad house with a steep red gable roof, brick foundation, and a large open porch under an overhanging roof.
description_view_0: The gable end of the house is visible, showing the wood plank siding and brick foundation, with no windows or doors.
description_view_1: This view highlights the open porch area under the roof extension, supported by wooden posts, and reveals the main entryway.
description_view_2: The opposite gable end is shown, similar to view 0, with plain wood siding and no visible openings.
description_view_3: A side view dominated by the long expanse of the red gable roof and wood siding, with no visible windows or doors.
attribute_entryway_design: Large open porch with wooden posts and a roof overhang, main entry likely under the porch, no visible decorative elements
